constraints.description: Activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.

Participation is limited to legal entities established in EU Member States, Iceland, Norway, and the following associated countries: Canada, Israel, Republic of Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
Entities controlled by non-eligible countries may be excluded unless appropriate guarantees are provided.
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA).
All proposals are expected to allocate tasks for cohesion activities with CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
All proposals are expected to incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Communicable results should be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
Projects selected in this topic should link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).
Maximum EU contribution per project is €19,000,000; minimum contribution is also set at €19,000,000 for this topic.

To ensure portfolio coverage, grants will be awarded not only in ranking order but also to at least one proposal focusing on each of the three sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving, subject to proposals passing all evaluation thresholds.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.

Financial support to third parties (FSTP) is permitted in this action with the following limits: maximum €300,000 per grant at stage 2; maximum €2,250,000 per grant at stage 3; maximum €2,550,000 per individual third party.

Maximum project duration is 48 months. This value is not explicitly stated in the topic text; 48 months is the standard maximum for large RIA actions under Horizon Europe. Verification against General Annex B is required.
constraints.assumption_note: The 48-month maximum project duration is not explicitly stated in the topic text per the notes field of selected_call.json. It is the standard maximum for large RIA actions under Horizon Europe and has been adopted as an assumption from the selected_call.json binding metadata. Verification against General Annex B for the applicable instrument constraints is required before finalising the project duration in the proposal.
